headline: Lululemon ends proxy battle with founder Chip Wilson
summary: STORY: Lululemon Athletica has ended its boardroom battle with founder Chip Wilson.The company agreed to give Wilson, who was CEO until 2005, two board picks in exchange for his pledge to stay quiet for around 18 months.The deal ends a messy proxy fight that Wilson started late last year, when he nominated three board directors and accused the company of having lost its "cool" factor.Lululemon in turn said Wilson had "outdated perspectives" about how to position the athleisure brand as it dealt with a drop in North American sales and a falling stock price due to increased competition.Lululemon said Wilson, who left the board in 2015 and owns 8.7% of the company, agreed to a contractual clause that caps his stake.Shares rose as much as almost seven percent in Wednesday morning trading after the company confirmed the story Reuters reported Tuesday.

raw.headline: Lululemon ends proxy battle with founder Chip Wilson
raw.summary: STORY: Lululemon Athletica has ended its boardroom battle with founder Chip Wilson.The company agreed to give Wilson, who was CEO until 2005, two board picks in exchange for his pledge to stay quiet for around 18 months.The deal ends a messy proxy fight that Wilson started late last year, when he nominated three board directors and accused the company of having lost its "cool" factor.Lululemon in turn said Wilson had "outdated perspectives" about how to position the athleisure brand as it dealt with a drop in North American sales and a falling stock price due to increased competition.Lululemon said Wilson, who left the board in 2015 and owns 8.7% of the company, agreed to a contractual clause that caps his stake.Shares rose as much as almost seven percent in Wednesday morning trading after the company confirmed the story Reuters reported Tuesday.

headline: Lululemon Board Shake-Up; Bath and Body Works Overhaul | Stock Movers
summary: On this episode of Stock Movers with Alexis Christoforous: - Dick's Sporting Goods (DKS) shares are lower after it forecast earnings per share for the full year of $13.27 to $14.27. - Lululemon (LULU) is up as it agreed to resolve a long-simmering dispute with founder Chip Wilson by overhauling its board with three new directors. The company will name two of Wilson's nominees to the board in Laura Gentile, a former ESPN executive, and Marc Maurer, the previous co-chief executive officer of sneaker maker On. A third new director will be announced by October 1. - Bath and Body Works (BBWI) shares are higher after it reported lower first quarter sales and says it's overhauling its strategy to return to growth. Sales were still better than expected.

The company will name two of Wilson's nominees to the board in Laura Gentile, a former ESPN executive, and Marc Maurer, the previous co-chief executive officer of sneaker maker On. A third new director will be announced by October 1. - Bath and Body Works (BBWI) shares are higher after it reported lower first quarter sales and says it's overhauling its strategy to return to growth. Sales were still better than expected.

headline: lululemon Enters into Cooperation Agreement with Chip Wilson; Laura Gentile and Marc Maurer to Join Company’s Board of Directors
summary: VANCOUVER, British Columbia, May 27, 2026--lululemon athletica inc. (NASDAQ:LULU) today announced that it has entered into a cooperation agreement with Dennis J. "Chip" Wilson, who owns approximately 8.7% of the company’s outstanding common stock. In connection with the agreement, Laura Gentile, former Chief Marketing Officer of ESPN, and Marc Maurer, former Co-Chief Executive Officer of On, will join the company’s Board of Directors following the company’s 2026 Annual Meeting of Shareholders. A
